schema.appDescription: Calculate advance tax liability and quarterly instalments for FY 2026-27. For self-employed, freelancers, and salaried with additional income.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Advance Tax Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option for your situation.

faq.q: Who must pay advance tax in India?
faq.a: Anyone with total tax liability above ₹10,000 after TDS must pay advance tax. This includes self-employed individuals, freelancers, traders, professionals, and salaried employees with significant non-salary income (rental, capital gains, interest).

faq.q: What are the advance tax due dates for FY 2026-27?
faq.a: Q1: 15 June 2026 (15% of liability), Q2: 15 September 2026 (30%), Q3: 15 December 2026 (30%), Q4: 15 March 2027 (25%). Senior citizens with no business income are exempt.

faq.q: What interest applies for missing advance tax deadlines?
faq.a: Missing advance tax instalments attracts interest under Section 234B (1% per month on unpaid tax) and Section 234C (1% per month on instalment shortfall). This interest is calculated at the time of filing your ITR.

faq.q: Is the Advance Tax Calculator free?
faq.a: Yes, the Advance Tax Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We do not store any financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the Advance Tax Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised and works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: Freelancer with ₹15 lakh annual income
examples.text: A freelancer earning ₹15 lakh in FY 2026-27 with no TDS has a tax liability of approximately ₹1.95 lakh under the new regime. Q1 payment (15 June) is ₹29,250; Q2 (15 Sep) ₹58,500; Q3 (15 Dec) ₹58,500; Q4 (15 Mar) ₹48,750.

examples.heading: Salaried employee with rental income
examples.text: A salaried employee whose employer deducts ₹80,000 TDS but earns ₹60,000 additional rental income may owe advance tax. Net tax after TDS is calculated, and 4 instalments are due if liability exceeds ₹10,000.
